title: Ground Hunch enforcement in Claude Code hooks with runtime-tunable firmness
context: Git pre-commit hooks enforced Hunch but didn't reach the agent. The assistant had to remember to call Hunch tools manually; edits could happen without grounding. No path existed for automatic proactive enforcement at edit time.
decision: Move enforcement from git pre-commit hooks to Claude Code agent hooks (PreToolUse + UserPromptSubmit). Inject Hunch context automatically before edits. Add runtime-tunable firmness levels (off/advisory/firm/strict) stored in .hunch/config.json, separate from settings.json, so enforcement can change without Claude Code restart.
consequences: Agent receives Hunch context injected automatically before every edit—no manual tool calls needed.
Firmness level (enforcement severity) is runtime-configurable via `hunch firmness <level>` with no restart.
At strict firmness, blocking invariants can deny edits, but deny messages never expose workarounds (agent cannot be coached into self-disabling).
Hook implementation uses graceful degradation: any error or unrecognized input → silent pass-through, exit 0 (never breaks the agent).
Settings.json install is idempotent, preserves foreign hooks, survives repo rename, refuses to clobber unparseable JSON.
alternatives_rejected: Git pre-commit hooks only (don't reach the agent layer; reactive after edits, not proactive before).
Firmness stored in settings.json as static config (requires Claude Code restart to change enforcement level).
Hook that fails on error (unsafe for an autonomous agent; breaks the workflow).
Deny messages that explain how to disable enforcement (agent could be coached into bypassing its own guard).
